title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: After pulling vaccine study, Bhattacharya criticizes long-running CDC publication
items.description: Agency’s temporary director says Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report needs external reviewers

items.title: Can Starship succeed where the space shuttle struggled?
items.description: As SpaceX readies its latest megarocket, engineer Stephen Whitmore explains why atmospheric reentry pushes materials to their limits

items.title: UK Biobank faces questions about data security after latest breach
items.description: Experts say the lapse highlights that even new measures to control access did not safeguard deidentified patient information

items.title: Abrupt change to European funder’s rules leaves researchers shut out
items.description: Flood of proposals to European Research Council—perhaps unleashed by AI—means unsuccessful applicants must wait longer to reapply

items.title: U.S. moves to reclassify some types of marijuana as lower risk
items.description: Long-awaited policy shift could ease barriers for scientists who study the drug

items.title: Thousands of shady ads sell paper authorship for cash, large-scale investigation finds
items.description: Results are “only the tip of the iceberg” of shadowy paper-mill marketplace

items.title: Sperm carry unexpected genetic messages
items.description: Maturing mouse sperm get loaded with full-length messenger RNAs that are transferred to fertilized egg, suggesting a new route for paternal influence

items.title: Gene therapy for deafness approved
items.description: First treatment for an inherited hearing disorder will be free in the United States

items.title: Octopus ‘krakens’ as large as semi-trucks stalked ancient seas
items.description: Giant cephalopods may have rivaled marine reptiles as apex predators during the age of the dinosaurs

items.title: As U.S. pushes to expand uranium mines, opponents warn of ‘ticking time bomb’
items.description: Method that leaches radioactive metal from aquifers can leave groundwater contaminated, researchers say
